title: 清洁女工之死
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\209-清洁女工之死-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
James Bentley discusses local residents with Hercule Poirot, recalling gossip shared by Mrs. MacKenzie. Bentley mentions Mr. Carpenter’s new house and his engagement to Mrs. Sherlock, noting Mrs. MacKenzie's disdain for her. He also recalls Mrs. MacKenzie’s negative views of the Weatherby couple, describing the wife as a "fuss" maker and the husband as silent, characterizing them as an unhappy family. During the conversation, Bentley reflects on life at Hunter's Manor and mentions helping a Silky Terrier caught in a trap. He also discusses a conversation with Dierdre Henderson, noting that her stepfather was involved in her mother's past difficulties.

In a separate setting at the post office, Mrs. Sweetman pressures Edna to report what she witnessed regarding the murder of Mrs. Upward. Distraught and fearful of her father's reaction, Edna refuses to speak to Bert Helling or a "foreign gentleman." Mrs. Weatherby enters the post office to buy knitting supplies, complaining about her recent ill health, her help's manners, and the local atmosphere of fear following the murder. She criticizes Mrs. Samohes of the Long Grass Inn for failing to lock her doors. Mrs. Sweetman informs Mrs. Weatherby that Mr. Upward is staying at the Long Grass Inn due to his distress, while Janet Groom has returned to her hometown. When Colonel Johnny Samoyski enters the post office to mail packages, Mrs. Sweetman asks him for advice regarding Edna and seeks to confirm a rumor that a lady was having coffee with Mrs. Upward on the night of the crime.
